terms.definition: The paid Elementor Pro plugin that extends the core editor with advanced features.

usage.when_to_use: When referring to the paid plan or features included in the Pro offering.

examples.correct: Upgrade to Editor Pro to access custom CSS and dynamic content.

terms.definition: A web property created and managed by a user using Elementor.

usage.when_to_use: In product UI, onboarding, settings, and instructional copy.
notes: 'Website' may be acceptable in marketing copy only

examples.correct: Choose a name for your site.
examples.avoid: Choose a name for your website.

avoid: editor (when referring to the ecosystem or product family)
terms.definition: A general term describing Elementor as a platform for building websites.

usage.when_to_use: When talking about Elementor at a high level or across multiple products.

examples.correct: Elementor is a website builder for creators.

avoid: builder (when referring to the specific product experience)
terms.definition: The specific Elementor product used to design and edit WordPress sites.

usage.when_to_use: When referring to the in-product editing experience.

examples.correct: The Elementor Editor gives you full visual control.

terms.definition: A named tier that defines features, limits, and entitlements.

usage.when_to_use: Describe what a user is on or can upgrade to; compare tiers or capabilities; in pricing and upgrade contexts.
usage.when_not_to_use: Referring to billing state or renewal mechanics.
notes: Plans define what you get, not how you pay

examples.correct: Your current plan includes custom fonts and dynamic content.

terms.definition: The ongoing billing relationship between the user and Elementor.

usage.when_to_use: Payments, renewals, invoices, cancellations, billing settings, and legal copy.

examples.correct: Your subscription renews on May 12.

terms.definition: Not used. Imprecise term inconsistent with Elementor’s billing and product models.

notes: Replace with 'subscription' or 'plan' depending on context

terms.definition: The open-source content management system Elementor is built for.

examples.correct: Elementor is the leading website builder for WordPress.

terms.definition: A UI element that appears over page content to display messages or collect input.

examples.correct: Create a pop-up to capture leads.
examples.avoid: Create a popup for email signup.

terms.definition: Elementor’s most comprehensive offering, providing the full website-building experience.

usage.when_to_use: When referring to the all-inclusive Elementor offering.
usage.when_not_to_use: As a generic adjective or lowercase.
notes: Treated as a proper product name

examples.correct: Upgrade to One for the complete website-building experience.
examples.avoid: Upgrade to one for everything Elementor offers.

terms.definition: Describes a bundled experience including multiple tools or capabilities in one.

examples.correct: One is an all-in-one solution for building websites.

terms.definition: Technology used to generate, improve, or automate outputs (text, design, etc.) in Elementor experiences.

examples.correct: Use AI to generate copy for your site.
examples.avoid: Use artificial intelligence to generate copy for your site.

terms.definition: An add-on extending functionality in WordPress or Elementor.

notes: Capitalize 'Plugin' at sentence start or in proper names

examples.correct: Install the plugin to start building your site.
examples.avoid: Install the plug-in to start building your site.

terms.definition: The process of moving a site and its subscription ownership to a client while the agency retains access.
A structured program status assigned to an agency based on eligibility and performance criteria.
Partner: an accepted agency in the program; Agency: a service provider that builds and manages client websites.
A centralized dashboard for monitoring, maintaining, and optimizing WordPress sites.

notes: Used in marketing, documentation, and cross-product contexts

examples.correct: Connect your sites to Manage.

terms.definition: The experience within Elementor One for monitoring, maintenance, and optimization of a site.

notes: Use in WordPress admin or One dashboard UI copy

examples.correct: Open Site management.

terms.definition: Elementor’s agentic AI that performs actions and creates assets inside WordPress.

examples.correct: Ask Angie.

terms.definition: A reusable UI element added to a page.

examples.correct: Rename widget.

terms.definition: A widget generated by Angie based on a user request.

examples.correct: Create custom widget.

terms.definition: A safe environment where Angie’s changes don’t affect the live site.

examples.correct: Enter Test mode.

terms.definition: Makes changes from Test Mode visible on your live site.

examples.correct: Publish to live.

terms.definition: The public-facing version of the website visitors see.

examples.correct: Affect your live site.

terms.definition: Angie’s proposed implementation before executing a request.

examples.correct: Review build plan.

terms.definition: An instruction given to Angie describing what to create or change.

examples.correct: Send request to Angie.

terms.definition: Angie attempts to automatically diagnose and repair an issue.

examples.correct: Fix with Angie.

terms.definition: Elementor’s next-generation editing experience built on a modern, CSS-based architecture enabling flexibility, scalability, and consistency.
Reusable elements that stay synced wherever they appear across a site.

examples.correct: Edit a Component once, and the changes update everywhere.
